channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: KnowledgeDeliver flaw exploited as a zero-day to install web shells
item.description: Hackers exploited a critical zero-day vulnerability in a server running the KnowledgeDeliver learning management system (LMS) to deploy the Godzilla web shell. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Feeding Frenzy: 'Megalodon' Malware Infects Thousands of GitHub Repos
item.description: In just six hours, the campaign quietly pushed thousands of malicious commits to more than 5,500 GitHub repositories, stealing credentials, developer secrets, and more....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Charter confirms data breach after ShinyHunters extortion threat
item.description: U.S. telecommunications giant Charter Communications has confirmed it suffered a data breach after the ShinyHunters extortion group threatened to leak stolen data unless a ransom is paid. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: The Hackers Behind Shai-Hulud: Lucky or Skilled?
item.description: TeamPCP, the hackers behind the Shai-Hulud worm, has done significant damage to the open source ecosystem. But it's not necessarily due to skill alone....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Microsoft Issues Out-of-Band SharePoint Patch
item.description: SharePoint access often means access to the keys of the kingdom, something attackers and defenders understand all too well....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: MuddyWater Uses DLL Side-Loading in Espionage Campaign Targeting 9 Countries
item.description:
The Iranian hacking group known as MuddyWater has been linked to a new campaign affecting at least nine organizations across nine countries on four continents in the first quarter of 2026.
The activi...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: How Varonis Atlas integrates Claude Compliance API for AI governance
item.description: AI governance requires visibility into how AI tools interact with enterprise data. Varonis explains how its Atlas platform uses Claude Compliance API data to help monitor usage, investigate risk, and ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft Defender can now automatically isolate hacked endpoints
item.description: Microsoft is testing a new Defender for Endpoint capability that will automatically isolate compromised endpoints to thwart attackers' attempts to move laterally across the network. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Webinar: Too many tools are slowing network incident response
item.description: IT teams often need to jump between monitoring dashboards, infrastructure tools, ticketing systems, and communication platforms during network incidents. This webinar explores how automation and AI-as...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Remembering Tim Wilson, Whose Legacy Lives on at Dark Reading
item.description: The co-founder and former editor-in-chief passed away five years ago in November. As Dark Reading enters its third decade, we pause to celebrate and honor Wilson's instrumental role in building and el...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: [THN Webinar] New AI DDoS Attacks Are Smarter. Learn How to Fight Back
item.description:
Every single day, hackers are finding new ways to crash websites and steal data.
But right now, something has changed. Hackers are no longer working alone. They are now using powerful Artificial Inte...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft Patches SharePoint RCE Flaw CVE-2026-45659 Across Server Versions
item.description: Microsoft has rolled out updates to fix a remote code execution vulnerability impacting SharePoint that could be exploited by bad actors in attacks without requiring any specialized conditions to be m...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: MFA Prompt Bombing: Why Your Second Factor Isn't Saving You
item.description: Multi-factor authentication (MFA) was supposed to close a critical gap in identity security. It meant that, even if an attacker possessed the account credentials, they couldn't log in without the seco...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CERT-In Recommends 12-Hour Patching for Internet-Facing Flaws Amid AI-Assisted Attacks
item.description: The Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) has issued new guidelines requiring organizations to patch critical security vulnerabilities in internet-exposed systems within 12 hours of being ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CISA orders feds to patch actively exploited Drupal vulnerability
item.description: CISA has given U.S. government agencies until Wednesday evening to secure their servers against an SQL injection vulnerability in the Drupal content management system (CMS) that it flagged as actively...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft: Domain Controller lookup may fail on Windows Server 2016
item.description: Microsoft has confirmed a new known issue affecting Windows Server 2016 systems that causes domain controller lookups to fail after installing the KB5087537 May 2026 security update. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Iranian Hackers Deploy MiniFast and MiniJunk V2 via Phishing and SEO Poisoning
item.description: The Iranian state-sponsored threat actor known as Nimbus Manticore (aka Screening Serpens and UNC1549) has been attributed to a fresh campaign using lures impersonating organizations in the aviation a...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: 7-Eleven data breach exposes personal information of 185,000 people
item.description: The ShinyHunters extortion gang stole the personal information of over 183,000 people after hacking the systems of convenience store chain giant 7-Eleven in April, according to data breach notificatio...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: KnowledgeDeliver LMS Flaw Exploited to Deploy Godzilla and Cobalt Strike
item.description: A now-patched high-severity security flaw affecting Digital Knowledge KnowledgeDeliver, a Learning Management System (LMS) popular in Japan, was exploited as a zero-day to deliver the Godzilla web she...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Anthropic’s restricted Claude Mythos model may be coming to Claude Code
item.description: Anthropic appears to be preparing for the public rollout of the Mythos model, which was announced in April as a restricted model that poses major security risks to private and public software. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Linux Flaws, Defender 0-Days, Router Botnets, and Supply Chain Chaos
item.description:
Monday recap. Same mess, new week.
A sketchy dev tool got people pwned, old bugs came back from the dead, and security products somehow needed protecting from themselves. A bunch of companies spent t...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
item.description: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and ...
item.dc:creator: Krebs on Security

item.title: FBI warns of Kali365 phishing service targeting Microsoft 365 accounts
item.description: The FBI is warning about the Kali365 phishing-as-a-service platform (PhaaS) that is used to hijack Microsoft 365 accounts by abusing OAuth device code authentication to steal session tokens and bypass...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Ghost CMS CVE-2026-26980 Exploited to Hijack 700+ Sites for ClickFix Attacks
item.description:
Threat actors are exploiting a recently disclosed critical security flaw in Ghost CMS to inject malicious JavaScript code with an aim to fuel ClickFix attacks.
According to QiAnXin XLab, the activity...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: The Alert Firehose Finally Meets Its Match
item.description: Ask a cybersecurity pro about Network Detection and Response (NDR) and you might still hear "Noisy," "Too much data." But ask the teams running NDR that includes agentic AI capabilities and you'll hea...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Lazarus Deploys RemotePE Memory-Only RAT Against Financial and Crypto Firms
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have shed light on a cross-platform malware called RemotePE that has been put to use by the North Korea-linked Lazarus Group in attacks targeting financial and cryptocurrency...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: TrapDoor Supply Chain Attack Spreads Credential-Stealing Malware via npm, PyPI, and CratesIO
item.description:
A new coordinated cross-ecosystem software supply chain attack campaign has targeted npm, PyPI, and Crates.io to distribute credential-stealing malware.
The campaign, codenamed TrapDoor, spans more t...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Ghost CMS SQL injection flaw exploited in large-scale ClickFix campaign
item.description: A large-scale campaign is exploiting a critical SQL injection vulnerability (CVE-2026-26980) in Ghost CMS to inject malicious JavaScript code that triggers ClickFix attack flows. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
